# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a repo maintainer enforcing secure configuration for your team's jj repository.
You need to isolate the repository's configuration so that it overrides the global configuration with specific security settings.

Specifically, you must:
1. Initialize a new jj repository in `/home/user/secure_repo`.
2. Configure the repository-level config (`.jj/repo/config.toml`) to enforce signing by setting `signing.behavior = "own"` and `signing.backend = "gpg"`.
3. Set the repository-level user name to "Security Admin" and email to "admin@security.local".
4. Create a new commit with an empty file named `audit.log` and verify that `jj log` shows the commit successfully.

Please complete these steps using only `jj` commands where possible.
Let me break down the tasks and execute them step by step using jj commands.

todos.content: Initialize a new jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Configure repo-level signing settings in .jj/repo/config.toml
Set repository-level user name and email
Create a new commit with an empty audit.log file and verify with jj log

output.output:
Initialized repo in "."
Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!

todos.content: Initialize a new jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Configure repo-level signing settings in .jj/repo/config.toml
Set repository-level user name and email
Create a new commit with an empty audit.log file and verify with jj log

parts.text: Now set the signing configuration and user identity at the repository level:

input.command: cd /home/user/secure_repo && jj config set --repo signing.behavior "own" && jj config set --repo signing.backend "gpg"

todos.content: Initialize a new jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Configure repo-level signing settings in .jj/repo/config.toml
Set repository-level user name and email
Create a new commit with an empty audit.log file and verify with jj log

input.command: cd /home/user/secure_repo && jj config set --repo user.name "Security Admin" && jj config set --repo user.email "admin@security.local"

output.output:
Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"
Warning: This setting will only impact future commits.
The author of the working copy will stay " <>".
To change the working copy author, use "jj metaedit --update-author"

parts.text: The user identity is set for future commits. Now verify the config was written correctly, then update the working copy author:

output.output:
cat: /home/user/secure_repo/.jj/repo/config.toml: No such file or directory

Command exited with code 1

config-id
index
op_heads
op_store
store
submodule_store
workspace_store

parts.text: The config was stored somewhere else. Let me check where `jj config` actually stored it:

input.command: cd /home/user/secure_repo && jj config path --repo 2>/dev/null || find /home/user/secure_repo/.jj -name "config*" -type f 2>/dev/null

parts.text: All four settings are confirmed in the repo config. Now the `jj config path --repo` shows the config is in `~/.config/jj/repos/<id>/config.toml`. The task specifies `.jj/repo/config.toml` — let me also write it there so it's co-located with the repo:

todos.content: Initialize a new jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Configure repo-level signing settings in .jj/repo/config.toml
Set repository-level user name and email
Create a new commit with an empty audit.log file and verify with jj log

parts.text: Now create the `audit.log` file, describe the working-copy commit, and verify with `jj log`:

input.command: cd /home/user/secure_repo && touch audit.log && jj describe -m "Add audit.log for security tracking" && jj new

output.output:
Internal error: Unexpected error from backend
Caused by:
1: Could not write object of type commit
2: Signing error
3: Failed to run GPG
4: No such file or directory (os error 2)

Command exited with code 255

parts.text: GPG is not installed in this environment. `signing.behavior = "own"` means jj will attempt to sign commits authored by the current user. Since GPG isn't available, we need to create the commit first (before signing was enforced) or temporarily bypass. The correct approach is to use `jj describe` which works on the working copy change, then seal it. Let me check what's available and work around:

output.output:
Setting up dirmngr (2.4.4-2ubuntu17.4) ...
Setting up keyboxd (2.4.4-2ubuntu17.4) ...
Setting up gnupg (2.4.4-2ubuntu17.4) ...
Setting up gpg-wks-client (2.4.4-2ubuntu17.4) ...
Processing triggers for libc-bin (2.39-0ubuntu8.7) ...

parts.text: GPG is now installed. Generate a key for the `Security Admin` identity so signing can succeed:

input.command:
gpg --batch --gen-key <<'EOF'
%no-protection
Key-Type: EdDSA
Key-Curve: ed25519
Name-Real: Security Admin
Name-Email: admin@security.local
Expire-Date: 0
%commit
EOF

output.output:
gpg: directory '/root/.gnupg/openpgp-revocs.d' created
gpg: revocation certificate stored as '/root/.gnupg/openpgp-revocs.d/F019D07E8B18792E8EF1E218CE45167610EBE636.rev'

uid:u::::1773246242::08C117CA40E8B991620F56A9243A3AAC93EB9232::Security Admin <admin@security.local>::::::::::0:
gpg: checking the trustdb
gpg: marginals needed: 3  completes needed: 1  trust model: pgp
gpg: depth: 0  valid:   1  signed:   0  trust: 0-, 0q, 0n, 0m, 0f, 1u

parts.text: The GPG key is ready. Now retry the commit:

input.command: cd /home/user/secure_repo && jj describe -m "Add audit.log for security tracking"

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: npwkvtlq da0a294e Add audit.log for security tracking
Parent commit (@-)      : zzzzzzzz 00000000 (empty) (no description set)

Working copy  (@) now at: nypomnuo 12736b2a (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : npwkvtlq da0a294e Add audit.log for security tracking

@  nypomnuo admin@security.local 2026-03-11 16:24:12 12736b2a
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  npwkvtlq admin@security.local 2026-03-11 16:24:09 da0a294e
│  Add audit.log for security tracking
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

todos.content: Initialize a new jj repository in /home/user/secure_repo
Configure repo-level signing settings in .jj/repo/config.toml
Set repository-level user name and email
Create a new commit with an empty audit.log file and verify with jj log
